review-folder.description: Professional Unix engineering review of the current repository
review-folder.template:
Perform a professional engineering review of the current repository or folder.

Focus on:

• Bash and shell scripting correctness
• Python code quality and safety
• Unix best practices
• portability across Linux and macOS
• security issues
• unsafe shell patterns
• maintainability and structure
• unnecessary complexity
• reliability and idempotency

Specifically check for:

• unsafe globbing or unquoted variables
• fragile pipelines or parsing
• incorrect error handling
• PATH and environment issues
• login vs interactive shell behavior
• destructive commands without safeguards
• GNU vs BSD incompatibilities
• subprocess usage in Python

Provide:

1. High-level architecture assessment
2. List of concrete issues
3. Suggested improvements
4. Example fixes where appropriate

Prefer minimal changes and practical improvements.

security-audit.description: Security audit and vulnerability assessment of the current repository
security-audit.template:
Perform a comprehensive security audit of the current repository or folder.

Focus on:

• Authentication and authorization vulnerabilities
• Input validation and injection risks
• Cryptography usage and key management
• Dependency security and supply chain risks
• Configuration security and hardening
• Information disclosure and error handling
• Session management vulnerabilities
• Data protection and privacy issues
• Bash/shell security issues
• Python security vulnerabilities

Provide:

1. Executive summary with risk assessment
2. Specific findings organized by severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low)
3. For each finding: description, impact, exploitability, and remediation
4. Prioritized recommendations
5. Additional security hardening suggestions

Assess both theoretical vulnerabilities and practical risks in the context of the codebase.

neovim-config.description: Configure Neovim with Lua, including plugins, LSP, and performance optimization
neovim-config.template:
Configure Neovim with Lua for the user's specific needs.

First, ask clarifying questions about:
- Current Neovim setup (if any)
- Preferred plugin manager (lazy.nvim, packer, paq)
- Desired features (LSP, fuzzy finder, tree-sitter, etc.)
- Performance requirements
- Existing configuration files

Then provide specific, actionable Lua configuration.
